context: You expect to pay your loan in the next upcoming month and you have started planning ahead, yet you hope someone can just give you 25 000$ to forget about it.

conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt the most connected to D, as our conversation was deeply personal—we shared our family struggles and dreams, like planning a trip to the Grand Canyon, which built a strong sense of mutual support. J also stood out, with our enthusiastic chat about their dream car and my loan creating a fun, motivating bond that made me feel genuinely excited to play alongside them. For a third, I connected well with G, discussing game strategies and building alliances based on trust, which felt collaborative and promising. On the other hand, I felt least connected to I, as our talk focused more on general budgeting tips and less on shared experiences, making it seem somewhat surface-level compared to the others.

Yes, I made a few agreements during the conversations, such as with F to keep an eye out for each other and with G to share notes on reliable players as we navigate the game. I trust D and J the most going into the game due to our heartfelt exchanges, but I don't specifically distrust anyone yet, though I'll remain cautious with those I connected with less, like I.
